content.thinking:
218.3 tok/s with reference at 237.0. That's 92.1% parity. The previous experiment had 215.0 tok/s at 90.3% parity. So the barrier removal gives a small consistent improvement (~2-3 tok/s).

218.3 vs previous ~213 average. It's a small gain but positive. The first run was 203.5 but the reference was also lower (211.5 vs 237), suggesting system contention. The second run at 218.3 with reference at 237.0 is more representative.

Let me keep this — it's a small but correct improvement, and the change is clean.

content.text: 218.3 tok/s with reference at 237.0 — consistent with a small improvement over the previous 213 average. The barrier removal is a clean, correct optimization. Keeping it.

input.summary: Removed 2 redundant bufferBarrier calls: one at end of encodeAttnProjFusedNorm and one at end of encodeAttnProjSeparateNorm. Both were duplicated by the caller encodeAttentionProjections. Saves 28 barriers per forward pass (one per decoder layer). Result: small but consistent improvement ~213 → ~218 tok/s. Parity with MLX reference improved from 90% to 92%.
